_doc: Controlled vocabulary of narration defect classes for the calibration loop. status: hard-gate (deterministic Python or a binding gate) | prompt-guidance (in a prompt/constitution but not enforced) | none. self_review_covered: does the self-review panel/gates reliably catch it (true|partial|false). fix_applied: date a calibration fix was applied (the loop then VERIFIES it by watching whether the class recurs in panel_misses). The reporter flags any class that shows up in panel/user MISSES while status!=hard-gate or self_review_covered!=true.

tired-landing.description: Close restates the body / bare 'Will you trust Him?' / portable to any episode.
tired-landing.where: generate LANDING TEST + judge LANDING + constitution

grace-trap.description: Verse-question turned on the viewer as a demand to PRODUCE the answer (secretly 'try harder to believe').
grace-trap.where: judge LANDING + constitution + NOW self-review G4

scene-scope-overclaim.description: Claim imports doctrine the scene hasn't reached (e.g. 'the One who made it' where the text shows only authority; pre-resurrection 'He is risen').
scene-scope-overclaim.where: constitution + generate LANDING + NOW self-review G1

kjv-verbatim-punctuation.description: Quoted KJV text differs from the source, including terminal punctuation (e.g. 8:27 ends with '!' not '?').
kjv-verbatim-punctuation.where: deterministic kjv_check._apply_kjv_gate overrides G1 in review + independent

shaming-the-person.description: Conviction shames the person in the text / the viewer toward performance ('Only His own people panicked' = law not grace).
shaming-the-person.where: series guardrail + tracker memory + NOW self-review G3

viewer-turn-missing.description: Landing stays third-person; the series signature (the question/scene turned onto YOU) is absent or only implicit.
viewer-turn-missing.where: NOW self-review G8(3) requires the turn EXPLICIT in the landing

self-help-payoff.description: Turn/landing resolves on 'your problem solved' rather than who Christ is.

clarity-conceit.description: Spine is a writerly conceit (geography/word-study/wordplay) rather than a felt truth a stranger gets on one hearing.

lecture-phrasing.description: Narrates the theology ('Notice', 'This teaches us', 'The point is') instead of showing it.

pacing-atempo.description: Narrator atempo > 1.30 (too many unspeedable quotes / too many words) -> rushed read.
pacing-atempo.where: per_turn_synth WARNS only, nothing blocks (open red-team finding)

causal-misattribution.description: Attributes a scene effect to the wrong cause (e.g. '#31: the light emptied the courtyard' when the accusers left by conscience in 8:9, before the 8:12 light-claim). A subtle exegetical error self-review passes.
causal-misattribution.where: NEW 2026-05-31 (caught on #31) — propose: G1/Theologian check that each cause->effect in the draft matches the pericope's actual sequence

dropped-grace-gift.description: Truncates the anchor verse before its own grace payoff (e.g. '#31: quoted John 8:12 but cut 'but shall have the light of life'), then hunts for grace elsewhere.
dropped-grace-gift.where: NEW 2026-05-31 (caught on #31) — propose: G4/Evangelist check whether the verse's own gospel payoff was quoted/used before importing one

disputed-passage-risk.description: Spine rests on a textually-disputed passage (e.g. John 7:53-8:11). Acceptable for SLK devotional, real risk for Awakeden apologetics.
disputed-passage-risk.where: NEW 2026-05-31 (caught on #31) — propose: brand-aware advisory flag (block for Awakeden, advise for SLK)
